clusters.title: Trump extends ceasefire, calls Iranian regime "seriously fractured"
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump announces extension of Iran ceasefire until ‘discussion concluded’
bullet_summary: President Trump threatened new attacks on Iran Tuesday and said he didn't want to extend the ceasefire
But later, he said he would extend the pause "indefinitely
He said Tehran had the upper hand militarily, including in the management of the strait of Hormuz, and would not allow Donald Trump to “create false narratives over the situation on the ground
President Trump said he would extend the ceasefire deadline with Iran at the request of Pakistan to allow the Iranian regime to “come up with a unified proposal

items.title: Trump extends ceasefire, calls Iranian regime "seriously fractured"
items.standfirst: President Trump threatened new attacks on Iran Tuesday and said he didn't want to extend the ceasefire. But later, he said he would extend the pause "indefinitely." Ed O'Keefe has the details.
items.content: President Trump threatened new attacks on Iran Tuesday and said he didn't want to extend the ceasefire. But later, he said he would extend the pause "indefinitely." Ed O'Keefe has the details.

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump unilaterally extends ceasefire; US navy continues blockade of Iranian ports
items.standfirst: US president moved off earlier threat to resume bombing, saying Pakistan officials requested ceasefire extension; military blockade on Iranian ports to continue, Trump saysIran’s armed forces are ready to deliver an “immediate and decisive response” to any renewed hostile action by its adversaries, Ali Abdollahi, commander of the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, was quoted by the Tasnim news agency as having said.He said Tehran had the upper hand militarily, including in the management of the strait of Hormuz, and would not allow Donald Trump to “create false narratives over the situation on the ground.” Continue reading...
items.content: US president moved off earlier threat to resume bombing, saying Pakistan officials requested ceasefire extension; military blockade on Iranian ports to continue, Trump saysIran’s armed forces are ready to deliver an “immediate and decisive response” to any renewed hostile action by its adversaries, Ali Abdollahi, commander of the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, was quoted by the Tasnim news agency as having said.He said Tehran had the upper hand militarily, including in the management of the strait of Hormuz, and would not allow Donald Trump to “create false narratives over the situation on the ground.” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump extends ceasefire deadline with Iran as talks stall
items.standfirst: President Trump said he would extend the ceasefire deadline with Iran at the request of Pakistan to allow the Iranian regime to “come up with a unified proposal." NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports.
items.content: President Trump said he would extend the ceasefire deadline with Iran at the request of Pakistan to allow the Iranian regime to “come up with a unified proposal." NBC News’ Keir Simmons reports.

items.title: Trump’s approval rating on US economy falls to 30 percent, new poll finds – US politics live
items.standfirst: Only 32 percent of respondents in new Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research poll approve of his leadership on Iran, which has remained at same levels since last monthSign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump said that he does not want to extend the two-week ceasefire with Iran, in an interview with CNBC. “I dont’ want to do that. We don’t have that much time,” the president said. The pause is set to expire tomorrow, and vice-president JD Vance will lead last-ditch talks in Islamabad today, in the hopes of striking a deal with Tehran.However, speaking to Joe Kernen, Trump said that he plans to resume strikes if negotiations collapse. “I expect to be bombing because I think that’s a better attitude to go in with,” the president added. “But we’re ready to go. I mean, the military is raring to go.” Continue reading...
items.content: Only 32 percent of respondents in new Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research poll approve of his leadership on Iran, which has remained at same levels since last monthSign up for the Breaking News US emailDonald Trump said that he does not want to extend the two-week ceasefire with Iran, in an interview with CNBC. “I dont’ want to do that. We don’t have that much time,” the president said. The pause is set to expire tomorrow, and vice-president JD Vance will lead last-ditch talks in Islamabad today, in the hopes of striking a deal with Tehran.However, speaking to Joe Kernen, Trump said that he plans to resume strikes if negotiations collapse. “I expect to be bombing because I think that’s a better attitude to go in with,” the president added. “But we’re ready to go. I mean, the military is raring to go.” Continue reading...

items.title: Trump announces extension of Iran ceasefire until ‘discussion concluded’
items.standfirst: Declaration comes amid intense efforts to bring two sides together in Pakistan for new round of talks Donald Trump unilaterally announced an extension of the two-week ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday amid frantic efforts to bring the two sides back to the negotiating table.Hours after announcing that he “expected to be bombing”, the US president said he would extend the ceasefire until Iranian negotiators submitted a proposal for peace. Continue reading...
items.content: Declaration comes amid intense efforts to bring two sides together in Pakistan for new round of talks Donald Trump unilaterally announced an extension of the two-week ceasefire with Iran on Tuesday amid frantic efforts to bring the two sides back to the negotiating table.Hours after announcing that he “expected to be bombing”, the US president said he would extend the ceasefire until Iranian negotiators submitted a proposal for peace. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire as peace talks hit impasse
items.standfirst: President’s latest mixed signal comes as US sends more troops to region
items.content: President’s latest mixed signal comes as US sends more troops to region

items.title: Trump extends ceasefire as uncertainty over U.S.-Iran peace talks remains
items.standfirst: With a two-week ceasefire set to expire and Iran balking at the resumption of peace talks, President Trump said he would be extending the deadline to allow for Iran to "come up with a unified proposal."
items.content: With a two-week ceasefire set to expire and Iran balking at the resumption of peace talks, President Trump said he would be extending the deadline to allow for Iran to "come up with a unified proposal."

items.title: JD Vance's Pakistan trip canceled after Trump announces ceasefire extension
items.standfirst: President Trump has extended the ceasefire with Iran. After the announcement, a White House official said the second round of in-person peace talks in Pakistan is on hold. Vice President JD Vance was expected to lead a delegation of U.S. negotiators to Islamabad on Tuesday. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy report.
items.content: President Trump has extended the ceasefire with Iran. After the announcement, a White House official said the second round of in-person peace talks in Pakistan is on hold. Vice President JD Vance was expected to lead a delegation of U.S. negotiators to Islamabad on Tuesday. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi and Courtney Kealy report.

items.title: Early details on Trump's extension of Iran ceasefire
items.standfirst: After days of mixed messages about the ceasefire with Iran, President Trump released a statement Tuesday with no ambiguity: He's extending the truce until a proposal from Tehran is submitted, and peace discussions conclude. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.
items.content: After days of mixed messages about the ceasefire with Iran, President Trump released a statement Tuesday with no ambiguity: He's extending the truce until a proposal from Tehran is submitted, and peace discussions conclude. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

items.title: Trump extends ceasefire, says giving Iran time to negotiate
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Tuesday extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, just hours before hostilities were set to resume, but he vowed to maintain a blockade of Tehran's ports. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the truce, crediting a request from mediator Pakistan and stressing the need to give Iran's "fractured" leadership time to form a proposal. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Tuesday extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, just hours before hostilities were set to resume, but he vowed to maintain a blockade of Tehran's ports. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the truce, crediting a request from mediator Pakistan and stressing the need to give Iran's "fractured" leadership time to form a proposal. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

items.title: Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely at request of Pakistan
items.standfirst: US president says on Truth Social attacks are on hold until Iran submits proposal and talks reach end Middle East crisis – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailDonald Trump announced in a social media post on Tuesday that he was indefinitely extending a ceasefire with Iran at the request of Pakistan, which has been mediating talks, until the country responded to the United States’ negotiating positions or until talks reached a dead end.“I have therefore directed our Military to continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able, and will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other,” the US president wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says on Truth Social attacks are on hold until Iran submits proposal and talks reach end Middle East crisis – live updatesSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailDonald Trump announced in a social media post on Tuesday that he was indefinitely extending a ceasefire with Iran at the request of Pakistan, which has been mediating talks, until the country responded to the United States’ negotiating positions or until talks reached a dead end.“I have therefore directed our Military to continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able, and will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other,” the US president wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump announces Iran ceasefire extension but says blockade remains
items.standfirst: US president says attacks will be held off until Iranian leaders 'come up with a unified proposal' to end war.
items.content: US president says attacks will be held off until Iranian leaders 'come up with a unified proposal' to end war.

items.title: US-Iran talks are in doubt as ceasefire nears an end
items.standfirst: US officials have announced that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold, as a two-week truce was set to expire and both countries warned that, without a deal, they were prepared to resume fighting. Iran said on Tuesday it had still yet to decide whether to attend the talks. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.
items.content: US officials have announced that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold, as a two-week truce was set to expire and both countries warned that, without a deal, they were prepared to resume fighting. Iran said on Tuesday it had still yet to decide whether to attend the talks. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

items.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
items.standfirst: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.
items.content: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.(Image credit: Aamir Qureshi)

items.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
items.standfirst: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.
items.content: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

items.title: Florida opens criminal probe into OpenAI over ChatGPT's alleged role in FSU shooting
items.standfirst: Attorney General James Uthmeier said his office launched a criminal investigation into OpenAI after reviewing conversation logs between ChatGPT and a Florida State University student accused of killing two people last year.
items.content: Attorney General James Uthmeier said his office launched a criminal investigation into OpenAI after reviewing conversation logs between ChatGPT and a Florida State University student accused of killing two people last year.

items.title: Florida to open criminal investigation into OpenAI over ChatGPT’s influence on alleged mass shooter
items.standfirst: State attorney general said inquiry will look into whether AI tool offered ‘significant advice’ to campus shooting suspectSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxFlorida’s top prosecutor is to launch a criminal investigation into how the tech company OpenAI and its software tool ChatGPT may influence users’ threats of harm to themselves or others, including whether it “offered significant advice” to a gunman accused of conducting a mass shooting in the state last year.State attorney general James Uthmeier said at a news conference on Tuesday that his office is expanding an examination of OpenAI, saying a “criminal investigation is necessary” and the state had issued subpoenas to the $852bn California-based tech firm. Continue reading...

clusters.neutral_headline: Two Israeli soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue in Lebanon village
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, Sky News World and others

items.title: Two Israeli soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue in Lebanon village
items.standfirst: Israel Defense Forces say the ‘soldiers’ conduct completely deviated from IDF orders and value’Two Israeli soldiers have been removed from combat duty and sentenced to 30 days in jail after one used a sledgehammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon while the other filmed him, the Israel Defense Forces have said.An image circulating on social media on Monday showed an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen from its cross in a Christian village in southern Lebanon, near the border with Israel, prompting outrage among Christian communities worldwide. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Olly Robbins’ account of Mandelson vetting piles pressure on Keir Starmer
clusters.neutral_headline: Olly Robbins’ account of Mandelson vetting piles pressure on Keir Starmer
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories, France24

items.title: Olly Robbins’ account of Mandelson vetting piles pressure on Keir Starmer
items.standfirst: Sacked civil servant tells select committee of ‘pressure’ to give clearance and ‘dismissive’ attitude to vettingThe civil servant sacked by Keir Starmer has given a devastating account of his government, saying Downing Street put huge pressure on the civil service to approve the appointment of Peter Mandelson as Washington ambassador despite the concerns of vetting officials.Olly Robbins, the former top official at the Foreign Office, said No 10 took a “dismissive” attitude to vetting, and Mandelson was given access to the Foreign Office building and to “higher-classification briefings” before he was granted security clearance. Continue reading...
items.content: Sacked civil servant tells select committee of ‘pressure’ to give clearance and ‘dismissive’ attitude to vettingThe civil servant sacked by Keir Starmer has given a devastating account of his government, saying Downing Street put huge pressure on the civil service to approve the appointment of Peter Mandelson as Washington ambassador despite the concerns of vetting officials.Olly Robbins, the former top official at the Foreign Office, said No 10 took a “dismissive” attitude to vetting, and Mandelson was given access to the Foreign Office building and to “higher-classification briefings” before he was granted security clearance. Continue reading...

items.content: Sacked Foreign Office chief describes 'dismissive' attitude to Mandelson vetting.

items.title: No 10 had 'dismissive attitude' to Mandelson vetting, says sacked Foreign Office chief
items.standfirst: Sir Olly Robbins has defended his decision to approve the peer's security clearance for the role.
items.content: Sir Olly Robbins has defended his decision to approve the peer's security clearance for the role.

items.title: UK: No 10 had "dismissive attitude" to Mandelson's vetting
items.standfirst: A former senior British Foreign Office official says he was under “constant pressure” from the Prime Minister’s office to speed up Peter Mandelson’s appointment as ambassador to the US, escalating a political row that is now threatening Keir Starmer’s leadership. Olly Robbins, the department’s most senior official until his dismissal last week, told a parliamentary committee that Number 10 showed a “dismissive attitude” toward security vetting for the appointment. It follows reports that independent officials recommended denying Mandelson clearance due to his past links to Jeffrey Epstein, prompting renewed calls for Starmer to resign after he insisted due process had been followed.
items.content: A former senior British Foreign Office official says he was under “constant pressure” from the Prime Minister’s office to speed up Peter Mandelson’s appointment as ambassador to the US, escalating a political row that is now threatening Keir Starmer’s leadership. Olly Robbins, the department’s most senior official until his dismissal last week, told a parliamentary committee that Number 10 showed a “dismissive attitude” toward security vetting for the appointment. It follows reports that independent officials recommended denying Mandelson clearance due to his past links to Jeffrey Epstein, prompting renewed calls for Starmer to resign after he insisted due process had been followed.

items.title: Key evidence from sacked official at heart of Mandelson vetting row
items.standfirst: Sir Olly Robbins has defended his actions amid a row over the vetting of the former US ambassador.
items.content: Sir Olly Robbins has defended his actions amid a row over the vetting of the former US ambassador.

clusters.title: Apple CEO Transition: Hardware, AI, China | Bloomberg Tech 4/21/2026
clusters.neutral_headline: Apple CEO Transition: Hardware, AI, China | Bloomberg Tech 4/21/2026
bullet_summary: I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release
Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Guardian Business, Wired

items.title: Apple CEO Transition: Hardware, AI, China | Bloomberg Tech 4/21/2026
items.standfirst: On this special edition of “Bloomberg Tech,” Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Apple’s CEO transition as the company announces hardware boss John Ternus will take over for Tim Cook on Sept. 1. Plus, Cook will become executive chairman, ensuring his corporate diplomacy skills and his strong ties with President Donald Trump remain on call for Apple. And, Ternus’ appointment signals Apple plans to stay focused on hardware. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: On this special edition of “Bloomberg Tech,” Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Apple’s CEO transition as the company announces hardware boss John Ternus will take over for Tim Cook on Sept. 1. Plus, Cook will become executive chairman, ensuring his corporate diplomacy skills and his strong ties with President Donald Trump remain on call for Apple. And, Ternus’ appointment signals Apple plans to stay focused on hardware. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Four key takeaways from Apple’s change of leadership
items.standfirst: Analysts say next boss John Ternus should diversify tech giant away from iPhones and raise its game in AIJohn Ternus takes over from Tim Cook as chief executive of Apple in September. A company insider, Ternus is moving up from his role as head of engineering to take control of the entire $4tn (£3tn) business.Apple is a vast, successful tech company and one of the most recognised brands in the world. But it faces challenges nonetheless. Here is a look at Ternus’s in-tray. Continue reading...
items.content: Analysts say next boss John Ternus should diversify tech giant away from iPhones and raise its game in AIJohn Ternus takes over from Tim Cook as chief executive of Apple in September. A company insider, Ternus is moving up from his role as head of engineering to take control of the entire $4tn (£3tn) business.Apple is a vast, successful tech company and one of the most recognised brands in the world. But it faces challenges nonetheless. Here is a look at Ternus’s in-tray. Continue reading...

items.title: Apple’s Cook Says He’s ‘Healthy’; Ternus Promises AI Products
items.standfirst: Apple Inc.’s Tim Cook, who’s stepping down as chief executive officer later this year, told employees on Tuesday that he is “healthy” and plans to serve as executive chairman for a long time.
items.content: Apple Inc.’s Tim Cook, who’s stepping down as chief executive officer later this year, told employees on Tuesday that he is “healthy” and plans to serve as executive chairman for a long time.

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
items.standfirst: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...
items.content: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down
items.standfirst: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.
items.content: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

items.title: Significance of Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick's resignation from Congress
items.standfirst: Democratic Florida Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigned from Congress on Tuesday, shortly before the start of a House Ethics Committee hearing to consider recommended punishment over allegations of campaign finance fraud. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.
items.content: Democratic Florida Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick resigned from Congress on Tuesday, shortly before the start of a House Ethics Committee hearing to consider recommended punishment over allegations of campaign finance fraud. CBS News' Taurean Small reports.

items.title: What led to Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick's resignation from Congress?
items.standfirst: Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida resigned from Congress on Tuesday ahead of a House Ethics Committee hearing to decide how to sanction her for more than two dozen campaign finance-related violations. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.
items.content: Democratic Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida resigned from Congress on Tuesday ahead of a House Ethics Committee hearing to decide how to sanction her for more than two dozen campaign finance-related violations. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more.

items.title: Florida Democrat Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, facing possible expulsion, resigns

items.content: The Florida Democrat faced a potential expulsion vote in the House as she prepares for a federal trial on allegations that she stole disaster funds and used some of the money to finance her campaign.(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)

items.title: DOJ charges Southern Poverty Law Center with fraud

items.content: A federal grand jury indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center on wire and bank fraud-related charges on Tuesday, the Justice Department says, accusing it of paying members of extremist groups as part of its efforts to investigate them.

clusters.title: 'Landmark' EU court ruling rejects Hungary anti-LGBTQ law
clusters.neutral_headline: EU court rules Hungary’s LGBTQ law violates human rights
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: 'Landmark' EU court ruling rejects Hungary anti-LGBTQ law
items.standfirst: The EU's top court ruled Tuesday that anti-LGBTQ legislation in Hungary breached the bloc's rules, in a move hailed as a "landmark" victory by Brussels. The European Commission, 16 of 27 member states and the European Parliament took Hungary to the European Court of Justice (ECJ) over the law, in what has been billed as the largest human rights case in the bloc's history. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.
items.content: The EU's top court ruled Tuesday that anti-LGBTQ legislation in Hungary breached the bloc's rules, in a move hailed as a "landmark" victory by Brussels. The European Commission, 16 of 27 member states and the European Parliament took Hungary to the European Court of Justice (ECJ) over the law, in what has been billed as the largest human rights case in the bloc's history. FRANCE 24's James Vasina reports.

items.title: EU court rules Hungary’s LGBTQ law violates human rights
items.standfirst: European Court of Justice says measure by PM Viktor Orban's government breaches EU law 'on a number of separate levels'.
items.content: European Court of Justice says measure by PM Viktor Orban's government breaches EU law 'on a number of separate levels'.

items.title: Hot-air balloon carrying 13 people lands in California back yard: ‘out of a fairytale’
items.standfirst: Pilot made emergency landing on small plot of grass at Temecula home amid low fuel and shifting windsA balloon landed in a southern California back yard – a balloon with 13 people.The enormous hot-air balloon, with a pilot and passengers in the basket, descended perfectly on Saturday on a small plot of grass at a home in Temecula. Hunter Perrin said he had no idea that he had visitors until a neighbor alerted him. Continue reading...

Hunter Perrin said he had no idea that he had visitors until a neighbor alerted him. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Top Law Firm Apologizes to Bankruptcy Judge for AI Hallucination

bullet_summary: Reported by Bloomberg Technology, Financial Times

items.title: Top Law Firm Apologizes to Bankruptcy Judge for AI Hallucination
items.standfirst: One of Wall Street’s prominent law firms, Sullivan & Cromwell, wrote to a bankruptcy judge to apologize for a court motion that included inaccurate citations generated by artificial intelligence, according to a filing in the US Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York.
items.content: One of Wall Street’s prominent law firms, Sullivan & Cromwell, wrote to a bankruptcy judge to apologize for a court motion that included inaccurate citations generated by artificial intelligence, according to a filing in the US Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York.

clusters.title: UK police make eight further arrests after arson attacks on Jewish sites
clusters.neutral_headline: UK police make eight further arrests after arson attacks on Jewish sites
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, NBC World

items.title: UK police make eight further arrests after arson attacks on Jewish sites
items.standfirst: British counter-terrorism officers have made a total of 23 arrests over a wave of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites and an Iran-critical media outlet in London, the Metropolitan police said Tuesday. Police are investigating whether Iran paid "criminal proxies" to carry out the attacks.
items.content: British counter-terrorism officers have made a total of 23 arrests over a wave of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites and an Iran-critical media outlet in London, the Metropolitan police said Tuesday. Police are investigating whether Iran paid "criminal proxies" to carry out the attacks.

items.title: U.K. probes shadowy Islamic group amid arson spree at Jewish sites
items.standfirst: After a spate of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, British authorities are investigating a shadowy online group with possible ties to Iran.
items.content: After a spate of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, British authorities are investigating a shadowy online group with possible ties to Iran.
